info.screen.roleid.civilian:
Civilian Camp
You have no special abilities; try to survive this journey.
info.screen.roleid.killer:
Killer Camp
You are a regular killer.
You have the basic killer shop.
Your goal is to eliminate all civilians.
info.screen.roleid.loose_end:
Neutral Camp
Has an independent win condition.
You receive 1 knife, 1 Dellinger pistol, 1 crowbar.
You have clown vision.
At spawn you gain shields based on player count (one layer per 6 players, up to 3 layers).
If you become a refugee, all lights are turned off at spawn.
Kill everyone with your weapons!!!
info.screen.roleid.vigilante:
Sheriff Camp
Start with a revolver.
Handcuffs can be purchased in the shop.
If you kill a civilian you will die for mis‑killing.
Handcuffs: Right‑click another player to restrain them; restrained players have limited movement and cannot use left‑click interactions.
Restrained players can hold the sneak key to break free.
info.screen.roleid.elf:
Sheriff Camp
You cannot pick up pistols; you start with a bow and can purchase crossbows, poison arrows, and light‑spirit arrows in the shop.
Poison Arrow: Anyone hit dies instantly.
Light‑Spirit Arrow: Anyone hit glows; if it hits a wall it shows the number of players within a 6‑block radius.
If you kill a good person you also die.
When the ranger dies the bow and crossbow turn into a revolver that drops on the ground.
info.screen.roleid.wind_yaose:
Neutral Camp
Win together with killers.
Permanent invisibility.
Has wind‑spirit vision.
Active skill: Lower volume of nearby players to 5%%.
Shop can purchase wind projectiles that push players away.
info.screen.roleid.blood_feudist:
Killer Camp
Only appears in games with 12 or more players.
Has unlimited stamina and fake moods.
Every time someone dies from mis‑killing a civilian you gain 100 gold.
Blood‑feudist evolution potion effects must be manually toggled with the skill key.
1 mis‑kill: Permanent speed 1
2 mis‑kills: Permanent haste 2
3 mis‑kills: Immediately gain 150 gold
4 mis‑kills: Permanent speed 2
5 mis‑kills: Immune to all negative effects
Blood‑feudist's mad mode has a shorter purchase cooldown.
Shop can only sell crowbars, lockpicks, and mad mode.
info.screen.roleid.photographer:
Civilian Camp
Can purchase instant‑camera and film in the shop.
When you press the shutter you can blind the person in front of you and reveal invisible people in the lens.
You can take photos to record crime scenes.
When you die you drop the photos you took.
In the inventory right‑click film onto the camera to load it.
Scroll wheel adjusts view distance, Shift adjusts flash and exposure, right‑click shoots.
Photos can be thrown with Q.
You drop photos on death.
info.screen.roleid.avenger:
Civilian Camp
Start with a protected target.
When the protected target dies you receive a revolver and learn the killer’s name.
The protected target may be good, neutral, or killer.
If the murderer is ??? the protected target dies from source‑less damage or from being too far away.
info.screen.roleid.conspirator:
Killer Camp
Shop has a death note that can guess other players’ professions; a correct guess causes the target to die of cardiac arrest.
If you guess the private detective’s identity you reveal your own location and information.
Shop item prices change and only notes, knives, guns, and lockpicks can be bought.
Three wrong guesses cause your own death.
info.screen.roleid.slippery_ghost:
Neutral Camp
Win together with killers.
Has a slippery‑ghost shop; passively gains 50 gold every 10 s.
Exclusive item – Empty Cartridge: Right‑click a target to put their gun on 30 s cooldown; invisible while held.
Exclusive item – Smoke Grenade: Right‑click to drop, creates smoke that blinds players inside; hitting a player clears their san (smoke lasts 10 s), invisible while held.
Item – Lockpick: Same effect as killers.
Destruction – Turn off lights: Same effect as killers.
info.screen.roleid.bomber:
Killer Camp
Has a bomb‑seller shop; can buy grenades, firecrackers, lockpicks.
Exclusive skill – Ascend: Use skill key to purchase a bomb, which is placed in the third inventory slot for 100 gold.
Purchased bomb makes a beeping sound after 5 s; while holding the bomb right‑click another player to pass it on!
Bombs are invisible to others.
info.screen.roleid.engineer:
Civilian Camp
Has an engineer shop; earn gold by doing tasks.
Can purchase crew keys.
Exclusive item – Reinforcement Tool: Can reinforce doors (prevents one crowbar) and is invisible while held.
Exclusive item – Alarm Trap (when a crowbar breaks the door it makes a sound; doors with alarm traps lock when the trap is placed), invisible while held.
Exclusive item – Lock: When a door is opened with a lockpick/crew key, the lockpicker must manually pick the lock (lock‑picking mini‑game); each failure has a 30%% chance to damage the lockpick/crew key (does not prevent crowbars).
Engineers can sneak‑right‑click doors while holding repair tools to take items off doors.
info.screen.roleid.boxer:
Civilian Camp
Skill: Steel‑Bone, press to gain 2.5 s invincibility. Cooldown 2 min.
Can purchase boxing gloves; enemies hit by the gloves are knocked back and stunned, and their items go on cooldown.
info.screen.roleid.postman:
Civilian Camp
Shop can purchase mail, which can be used to exchange one item with another player; it disappears after one use.
info.screen.roleid.detective:
Civilian Camp
Point at a player and press the skill key to view their inventory UI.
If the player moves the UI closes; cooldown 60 s.
Crouching continuously lets you hear nearby noises.
When a conspirator guesses you, you learn their name and location.
info.screen.roleid.stalker:
Killer Camp
Simple description
Press the skill key at the start of a crowd to accumulate energy; the more people, the faster energy accumulates.
When energy is full you gain a knife; knives bought from the shop can be placed in the off‑hand.
Holding the right‑click then thrusts at the target; main‑hand knife and off‑hand knife cooldowns are independent (dual‑wield possible).
After three phases you can press Q while holding the knife to dash.

Detailed description
Phase 1 – [Cannot purchase from shop, can only buy lockpicks]
1. Unlimited sprint, has one shield layer.
2. Press skill key to absorb energy until entering Phase 2.

Phase 2
1. Hunting Dagger: Gain a knife; shop can buy a second knife to place in off‑hand; when main‑hand is empty the off‑hand knife is hidden. Right‑click to charge; when sneaking charge time is 0.1 s; when fully charged if someone is in front it automatically releases and puts the main‑hand knife on cooldown. If off‑hand knife is not on cooldown, right‑click can use the off‑hand knife to thrust. Cooldown 20 s.
2. Cannot sprint.
Phase 3 condition: Kill a certain number of players with the dagger and fill energy.

Phase 3
Unlock dash; can dash while holding the knife without sneaking, press Q to dash along the Y‑axis, cooldown 3 s; only sneakers can dash on the ground.
info.screen.roleid.athlete:
Civilian Camp
Unlimited stamina.
Use skill to gain speed 3, cooldown 100 s.
Can purchase splash‑type speed 3 in the shop.
info.screen.roleid.admirer:
Neutral Camp
Win together with killers.
You have clown vision and can purchase crew keys.
Use skill to peek at highlighted target and gain energy.
When reaching 90 %% you become a manipulator.
info.screen.roleid.trapper:
Killer Camp
Use skill aimed at the ground to set traps (crouch + skill key switches trap type).

【Catastrophe Trap】 Hidden trap; when triggered it makes a loud noise, reveals the target’s location, and briefly restricts target movement. Repeated triggers on the same player increase control time (3 s → 10 s → 25 s).

【Tripwire Trap】 Visible trap. When a target walks through it they receive 3 s Slow I; if the target is sprinting they fall, receive 5 s Slow IV and have stamina cleared.
info.screen.roleid.star:
Civilian Camp
Skill makes everyone within 30 blocks look at them and glow for two seconds.
You can see how many people around are looking at you.
Each person looking at you gives you +10 , up to 150 .
Can purchase signature paper; right‑click to sign a normal signature.
Players with a normal signature can continuously see the star’s position.
Players with a signature gain Speed I when the star is in their view.
Crouch + right‑click to sign a death‑warrant (once only).
Players with a death‑warrant can block one fatal hit for you when they take lethal damage, you take the damage instead.
info.screen.roleid.veteran:
Civilian Camp
Start with a bayonet, same effect as killers; disappears after one use – cooldown 60 s; good people hit by the bayonet receive a small brain penalty.
Using the bayonet triggers the clown’s mad mode but does not consume bayonet uses.
Veterans can purchase a bayonet in the shop.
info.screen.roleid.singer:
Civilian Camp
Shop sells four types of records.
Buying a record provides different buffs/debuffs to nearby players:
- Speed I
- Restore 1%% san per second
- Slow I
- Mining Fatigue V (has a special effect when killers enter berserk mode)
info.screen.roleid.psychologist:
Civilian Camp
When your san is at 70, use skill aimed at a person; the target must stay still; after ten seconds you can fully restore their san.
Passive aura: while alive, allies within 4 blocks continuously receive 5s Low-SAN Shader Resistance II and Mood Drain Reduction I.
Shop items: Mint Candies (75 gold) and Vitamin (125 gold).
Mint candies can be thrown with Q.
Vitamin: Right-click a target to apply one random buff from 6 configurable effects; the target receives a message showing which effect was applied.
info.screen.roleid.puppeteer:
Neutral Camp
Win together with killers.
Simple description
Right‑click a corpse to collect; after collecting enough you advance to the next phase.
In the next phase press skill key to release a puppet; when the puppet dies it returns to the body; the puppet is a random killer role (excluding the post‑mortem killer).
During puppet release the puppeteer’s body stays in place.

Detailed description
Phase 1 – Camp: Neutral
1. Can right‑click a corpse to make it disappear, reclaim it.
Evolution condition: collect enough corpses then become a killer (excluding puppeteer) [Number of corpses needed = total players ÷ 6, corpse‑collect skill has a 10 s cooldown].
2. Puppeteer renders as rainbow in killer vision.

Phase 2
1. Cannot reclaim corpses.
2. Use skill to release and control a puppet [number of uses depends on collected corpses].
3. Killer‑camp body cannot purchase from shop.
4. Puppet death does not truly kill it; it returns to body view [if body is alive].
5. While controlling puppet, if the puppeteer’s body dies, the puppet is cleared and the puppeteer truly dies.
6. Controlling a dummy randomly changes its role to a random killer role.
7. Puppet control is limited to one minute; after one minute it switches back to Phase 2.
8. Switching back to body resets role to puppeteer.
9. Puppet and body have independent inventories.
10. Creating a puppet has a cooldown.
info.screen.roleid.better_vigilante:
Civilian Camp
Passive skill: Last‑Stand
When only 2 good people remain, you receive a Dellinger pistol and grenades, and unlock vision.
Dellinger pistol: Bullets only regenerate on hit; cooldown 2 s; no brain penalty.
info.screen.roleid.best_vigilante:
Sheriff Camp
Easter‑egg role, only 0.1%% chance to appear.
Start with a hand grenade.
Only spawns in games with 12 or more players.
You cannot become a better vigilante by any means other than natural spawn.
info.screen.roleid.baseball_player:
Sheriff Camp
Easter-egg role, only 1%% chance to appear.
Start with a baseball bat.
info.screen.roleid.creeper:
Killer Camp
Easter-egg role, only 10%% chance to appear.
Infinite stamina, can only buy lockpicks and knives (130 gold).
Press skill key to spend 300 gold to ignite yourself, explode after 10s, killing yourself and players within 4 blocks.
info.screen.roleid.writer:
Civilian Camp
Easter‑egg role, only 2%% chance to appear.
You have an exclusive shop where you can buy books and pens.
Finished books can be given to others to read; others can complete reading tasks by reading the author’s book.
After death you drop your books, pens, and finished books.
info.screen.roleid.telegrapher:
Civilian Camp
Easter‑egg role, only 2%% chance to appear.
Press the skill key to open the telegram UI and send an anonymous message to all players.
You can send up to 6 telegrams per game.
Telegram messages appear as titles on all players’ screens.
info.screen.roleid.rescuer:
Civilian Camp
Can purchase rope and body bag.
Rope: Has 2 durability; pulls the nearest player within 12 blocks to you; every right-click triggers a 3 s cooldown, while a successful pull in non-creative mode applies a 5 s cooldown and consumes 1 durability.
Body bag: Can clear corpses.
info.screen.roleid.firefighter:
Civilian Camp
Can purchase fire axe and extinguisher.
Fire axe and extinguisher can each be purchased only once globally.
- Fire axe: Has 3 durability; crouch + right‑click a door to pry it open and consume 1 durability.
  Hold right‑click to raise the axe and slash (only when fully durable; after slashing it consumes all 3 durability).
- Extinguisher: Right‑click to spray at people (consumes 1 durability per use; can hold right‑click to spray continuously up to 5 s).
  Sprayed players receive Slow and Blind (1.5 s).
  Extinguisher can clear gasoline from poisoned players.
info.screen.roleid.awesome_binglus:
Civilian Camp
Start with a sticky note.
You can stick the note on other players.
Shop can purchase sticky notes.
You can see whether anyone within 5 blocks has died in the last 30 s (the deeper the glow, the longer ago the death).
info.screen.roleid.bartender:
Civilian Camp
Can temporarily see through players who have drunk a beverage.
Can see whether food or drink is poisoned.
Can see whether a player is poisoned.
Shop can purchase shield reagent; right‑click a food/drink plate to give the next consumer a shield that blocks one fatal hit (does not work against brain damage).
Players with a shield are shown with a blue border in the bartender’s view.
Players with both shield and poison show a yellow‑green border.
info.screen.roleid.coroner:
Civilian Camp
Can see deceased information (cause of death, time of death) and can detect fake corpses.
Periodically can sense nearby corpses.
info.screen.roleid.doctor:
Civilian Camp
When a doctor is present, the killer side always has a poisoner.
You can detect nearby poisoned players (you receive a text tip when someone nearby is poisoned).
Start with a single‑use heart pacemaker (cannot be seen by other players).
Heart pacemaker: Use on a living player; the used player receives a notification. If the player dies within 120 s, they revive after 30 s.
Syringe: Use on others to treat poisoned people; after death the syringe is passed to another civilian player.
Pill: Consuming cures poison; can be given to others.
Purify bomb: Throws and detoxifies players within a 3‑block radius.
Can spend 50 gold to buy a poison‑test reagent; the reagent can check if a target is poisoned.
When the doctor is alive, players who die within 45 s cannot use killer intuition and have limited vision for 45 s after death!
info.screen.roleid.noisemaker:
Civilian Camp
Voice range is greatly increased; after death the corpse glows.
Using the skill increases your speaking range fourfold.
When you trigger the skill, players within 5 blocks (excluding loud‑voiced ones) glow.
info.screen.roleid.recaller:
Civilian Camp
Use skill to mark a location; use again to spend 100 gold to return to the marked spot.
info.screen.roleid.voodoo:
Civilian Camp
Can bind one person; when you die (except from environmental damage) the bound person also dies.
§e!!!§r Sheriff killing a voodoo does not trigger punishment.
§e!!!§r Shield prevents voodoo activation.
info.screen.roleid.jester:
Neutral Camp
Win together with killers.
Start with a fake gun and fake knife, have clown vision; when killed by a good person you trigger berserk (killer/neutral kills do not trigger berserk).
Clown berserk has its own background music.
Can purchase fake poison; fake poison can be triggered by the poisoner’s catalyst to be lethal.
info.screen.roleid.vulture:
Neutral Camp
Win together with killers.
Use skill to eat corpses; information of eaten corpses is erased (the coroner sees gibberish).
One corpse must be eaten per six players.
After eating a corpse you gain a gray particle effect, so avoid eating corpses in public.
After reaching the required number you become a random killer.
When turned into a killer you inherit 40%% of the vulture’s gold.
info.screen.roleid.executioner:
Killer Camp
You cannot buy knives or mad mode. Your gun is extremely cheap.
You cannot distinguish good from bad via vision.
When you kill a player with a gun there is a chance the gun does not drop.
You start with a locked target (highlighted with a yellow border); after the target dies the executioner gains fifty gold.
The executioner cannot kill players other than the target; after the target dies a new target is selected.
Note: Your grenades can only kill your target and have no effect on others.
Executioner can buy Shooting Frenzy: during berserk shooting cooldown -50%%, gun does not drop and you can hold two guns, not affected by locked target.
info.screen.roleid.morphling:
Killer Camp
Use skill to change skin – duration 35 s, CD 20 s.
Can purchase hallucinogenic bottle to create a fog that blinds nearby people and forces a view change. Invisible while held.
info.screen.roleid.phantom:
Killer Camp
Use skill to become invisible; items in hand are not hidden, you still have collision while invisible.
info.screen.roleid.ghost:
Civilian Camp
Cannot be seen by killer intuition (but can be seen by some neutral vision).
You can view remaining game time.
At 3 minutes left you unlock a skill; spend 150 gold to become invisible, you still have collision while invisible.
When only one tiny transparent remains, game time fast‑forwards to 2 minutes.
info.screen.roleid.gambler:
Neutral Camp
Has an independent win condition.
Every 30 s draws a profession; use skill key to select.
Can pick up a pistol.
Can use pistol while crouching to suicide and become the selected profession.
After being killed by someone there is a 33%% chance to become a killer‑camp profession,
33%% chance to become a sheriff‑camp profession,
33%% chance to die,
1%% chance to win independently.
info.screen.roleid.attendant:
Civilian Camp
Shop can purchase crew keys, iron door keys, flashlights.
Crew key: Can open dormitory doors, usable 5 times.
Iron door key: Can open iron doors, usable 3 times.
Flashlight: Right‑click to illuminate the area ahead.
Press skill key to turn on lights within 5 blocks (can turn on lights when they are off).
When aiming at a player you can see the player’s room number.
Active skill – Turn on lights: Press skill key to ignore conditions and turn on lights within 5 blocks.
info.screen.roleid.swapper:
Killer Camp
Open inventory to select a player then swap the positions of two players.
Activating the skill has a 2.5 s delay during which the swapper cannot move.
Swapper cannot swap others while falling.
Swapped players receive a notice.
info.screen.roleid.conductor:
Civilian Camp
Start with a master key and a telescope.
Telescope can view player names from afar.
Master key can open all doors without limit.
When you die the master key and telescope drop.
Shop can purchase master keys and screwdrivers.
Captain’s screwdriver can only be used to remove reinforced items from doors (crouch + right‑click door to remove).
info.screen.roleid.the_insane_damned_par:
Killer Camp
You can see the dead‑talk channel chat.
You can activate skill to disguise as a corpse.
Disguised corpses are detected by the coroner, and you are not invincible while disguised.
Post‑mortem killer enters a fake‑death state after death.
Post‑mortem killer can climb onto another corpse to revive (once per game).
info.screen.roleid.broadcaster:
Civilian Camp
You start with 200 gold; you can spend 100 for a global broadcast.
You can edit broadcast messages at any time; messages persist until successfully sent.
When you die, if there is a pending message it is automatically sent.
All players will learn of your death.
You can view remaining game time.
info.screen.roleid.manipulator:
Killer Camp
You can manipulate a target player for a few seconds.
Manipulated players move randomly and cannot use items.
info.screen.roleid.poisoner:
Killer Camp
When you appear on the killer side, there will always be a doctor on the good side.
You are immune to poison and can see poisoned players; you can buy poison needles and purchase poison and scorpions at lower prices.
Poison needle is a ranged weapon (range: 15 blocks, charge 0.25 s); right‑click to aim; hit player will be poisoned and die after 30 s, single‑use.
Can purchase a catalyst; right‑click to make all poisoned players die instantly (including clown’s fake poison).
Fake pill: Your pills are poisonous and will poison anyone who eats them; can be given to others.
Chlorine bomb: Throws and poisons players within a 3‑block radius.
Poison: Can be placed on food/drink plates; the next player to consume becomes poisoned.
Scorpion: Can be placed on beds; players sleeping on the bed get poisoned; scorpion poison spreads to nearby beds.
info.screen.roleid.thief:
Neutral Camp
Has an independent win condition.
You can crouch and press the skill key to select mode: steal money, steal items, sell items; press skill key on a player to use the skill (skill cooldown 30 s; stealing failure does not trigger cooldown; selling has no cooldown).
When the thief’s gold reaches a certain amount (total players × 55 gold at game start) it automatically converts to gold ingots.
Each theft steals 40%% of the target’s gold (minimum 100 gold); if the target has less than 100 gold you cannot steal; the stolen gold is subtracted from the target.
Stealing items steals a random item from the target’s inventory (cannot steal letters, food, drink, or civilian pistols).
Selling mode lets you sell held items for 50 gold.
Killing a player gives the thief 200 gold.
Victory condition: thief holds a gold ingot and returns to a room to sleep.
info.screen.roleid.candlebearer:
Neutral Camp
Has an independent win condition.
Only appears in games with 12 or more players.
Killers cannot detect you through instinct.
When instinct is enabled, you can see all targets; candle-lit players and their corpses use the Candle Bearer color, while unlit targets appear gray.
Hold a candle to dedicate it to living players; the lit player and their body glow after 4 seconds.
You gain 1 invisibility charge only after successfully dedicating the linked corpse, up to 3 charges total. Press the skill key to turn invisible for 12 seconds.
Successfully dedicating to the linked corpse also increases your progress.
Target progress is floor(player_count / 5) + 2, with a minimum of 4.
info.screen.roleid.amnesiac:
Neutral Camp
Before changing identity you win with good people.
You can see corpses around you.
Before stealing a profession you must do tasks to maintain san.
Right‑click a corpse to obtain its profession.
Your win goal matches the stolen profession’s win goal.
info.screen.roleid.arsonist:
Neutral Camp
Has an independent win condition.
Simple description
Right‑click a player with a gasoline can to douse them.
When enough players are doused, the lighter goes on cooldown; after cooldown lighting the gasoline does not kill you.
Douse everyone to win.

Detailed description
Right‑click a player with a gasoline can to douse them; right‑click lighter to ignite gasoline.
If not enough players are doused, lighting the gasoline kills you.
When the lighter is on cooldown, after cooldown lighting gasoline does not kill you.
Arsonist appears as good in killer intuition.
Note: Others can see your gasoline can and lighter.
If you burn everyone you win.
info.screen.roleid.avaricious:
Killer Camp
You start with only 50 gold.
Every minute the pickpocket steals gold from nearby players.
Each player can provide a certain amount of gold to the pickpocket.
Stolen gold varies with nearby player count and distance between pickpocket and players.
info.screen.roleid.initiate:
Neutral Camp
Before changing identity you win with good people, but your goal is to become a killer.
Simple description
(like a dueling goose‑duck kill in Goose Duck)
Do tasks, earn money to buy a knife to knife another beginner.
You can only see the other beginner’s position after buying a knife.
You personally kill the other beginner and become a killer.
If the other beginner dies from non‑player causes you become an amnesiac.
If the other beginner is killed by someone else you become a camp hostile to them (killed by neutral becomes random neutral).
If you knife a non‑beginner you die.

Detailed description
Two beginners appear per game, only in games with 12 or more players. If only one beginner is present you become an amnesiac after 5 s.
You can only see the other beginner’s position after buying a knife.
You can buy a knife for 200 gold; this knife can only be used to knife the other beginner.
If you knife anyone else you die for failing the test.
You are neutral but win with good people.
You cannot pick up guns; you are not penalized by brain when killed by good people.
When you successfully knife the other beginner you become a random killer.
If the other beginner dies from non‑player causes you become an amnesiac.
If the other beginner is killed by a killer you become a random good‑person camp.
If the other beginner is killed by a good person you become a random killer camp.
If the other beginner is killed by neutral you become a random neutral camp.
If the other beginner is killed by a good person you become a random killer camp.
If the game ends with good people winning and both beginners alive they win together.
After the assassination your win goal matches the killer’s.
If the other beginner dies from homicide or suicide your win goal matches the identity you became.
info.screen.roleid.necromancer:
Killer Camp
You have the ability to resurrect the dead.
For each killed killer on the field you gain one resurrection chance.
Right‑click a corpse to resurrect it as a normal killer and consume one resurrection chance (cooldown: 3 min).
You cannot purchase any items or weapons except lockpicks, but you still have killer vision.
When resurrecting a dead, a totem sound plays and it is announced to the whole server.
info.screen.roleid.recorder:
Neutral Camp
Has an independent win condition.
Only active in games with 10 or more players.
You have killer vision.
Start with a notebook to guess player identities.
The notebook records all professions and player information and is invisible to others.
It is recommended to quickly record neutral roles that can change your own profession.
After guessing enough players you win.
If you record wrong a certain number of times you die.
info.screen.roleid.commander:
Neutral Camp
Win together with killers.
Commander starts with a fake knife and fake gun.
Active – you can switch to the killer channel; only killers can hear and see your messages on this channel.
On this channel you also receive killer messages (but they may be heard/seen by nearby players).
Passive – Alertness: When you open intuition you can see players within 5 blocks who hold guns and sheriff‑camp players; they appear blue.
info.screen.roleid.fortuneteller:
Civilian Camp
Can spend money to divine a player’s fate; when the divined player is about to die they teleport to you and avoid death (duration 120 s).
Skill enters cooldown after use.
info.screen.roleid.oldman:
Civilian Camp
Your base movement speed is slower but you do not need to complete running tasks.
You can purchase a wheelchair in the shop; wheelchair durability is 90 s; right‑click the wheelchair to deploy.
Wheelchair can push other players.
While seated in the wheelchair press skill key to retrieve the wheelchair back to inventory.
info.screen.roleid.magician:
Civilian Camp
Killers will mistakenly think you are a killer ally; you will randomly play as a killer at the start of the game.
Killers see your identity as the killer you are playing; the announcer counts the magician as a killer.
All items purchasable in the shop are fake (except firecrackers and sticky notes).
Fake mad mode: Enter mad mode but wield a fake bat; the fake bat only knocks back; other good people do not die from the magician’s fake mad mode shield (magician’s fake mad mode can switch held items).
If the commander is present, the magician joins the commander’s channel.
info.screen.roleid.bandit:
Killer Camp
Bandit’s killer intuition only sees players within a 10‑block radius, but sees killer teammates without distance limit.
Starts with a bandit pistol and a crowbar.
Passive skill: After bandit kills someone they can steal half of the victim’s gold; the killed player loses half of their gold.
Bandit shop can purchase: knife, bandit pistol, grenade, turn off lights, flashbang, decoy bomb.
Flashbang: Throwable; upon landing causes flash effect to players within 6 blocks looking at the flashbang.
Decoy bomb: Throwable; after being thrown it continuously makes gunfire sounds along its trajectory.
Cannot purchase mad mode, lockpicks, or crowbars.
info.screen.roleid.cleaner:
Killer Camp
Carries a bucket of sulfuric acid; the bucket can clear a corpse.
Releasing skill clears dropped items within a certain range and plays a sound.
Scavenger can pick up guns but cannot purchase revolvers, mad mode, grenades, or turn off lights in the shop.
info.screen.roleid.chef:
Civilian Camp
Can purchase pot, snacks and water in the shop (snacks and water can be thrown).
Chef can temporarily see players who have eaten food.
Every completed task gives a food ingredient; two ingredients plus a food item from a plate can be cooked in the pot to make a dish (dishes can be thrown).
Dish potion effects do not show an icon but are effective.
Cooking mini‑game: Press A and D to move the pot left/right, catch falling ingredients.
Ingredients falling in the mini‑game give the dish different buffs/debuffs.
Buffs include: skip current task, reduce item cooldown, night vision, speed 1, restore san, add gold.
Debuffs include: glow, slow 1, darkness, nausea.
Larger ingredients increase effect duration (can stack).
Avoid adding rotten meat, spider eyes, poisonous potatoes, glowing berries to the pot.
info.screen.roleid.clockmaker:
Civilian Camp
Only appears in games with 12 or more players.
You can view the game time.
Press skill key to spend 125 gold, reduce game time by 45 s and speed up world time by 2000 ticks.
Speeding up game time makes all skill cooldowns faster.
You, who are good with time, ignore any time‑stop effects.
Game time can be reduced to a minimum of 1 min 30 s.
Shop can purchase clocks and pocket watches. Both can be dropped (can be given to others to view).
Clock: View world time. Pocket watch: Right‑click to view current game time (60 s cooldown).
info.screen.roleid.wayfarer:
Neutral Camp
Has an independent win condition.
The Red‑Dust Guest is not a normal traveler; they start without keys or letters, and have a fake knife and fake gun.
The Red‑Dust Guest can approach corpses to read extra killer information (affected by vulture and arsonist); then can choose to activate skill (right‑click corpse), activate fake knife to become a one‑time knife, obtain the corpse’s key and letter (triggers once).
Phase 1 – Hunt Killer: Find the corpse’s killer and kill them with the one‑time knife (mistake kills you).
Phase 2 – Exit Play: After killing, reenact the corpse’s death.
Phase 3 – Red‑Dust Dream: Sleep on a bed to achieve independent win.
Tip: After Phase 2 the Red‑Dust Guest does not die directly but is teleported to the corpse used for skill activation; the Red‑Dust Guest needs to kill the original killer of the corpse.
info.screen.roleid.monitor:
Civilian Camp
You can use the skill key to mark a player and see their position.
Passive – you can sense whether any player has died in the game, cooldown 60 s.
Shop can view passive cooldown.
info.screen.roleid.patroller:
Sheriff Camp
When someone dies within 30 blocks of your vision you receive a patrol gun and a crew key.
Shop can purchase revolvers.
Patrol gun: Has independent cooldown, can exist alongside revolver.
Patrol gun cooldown is 1/5 of normal revolver.
Crew key: Can open any room door, usable 5 times.
info.screen.roleid.swast:
Sheriff Camp
Start with a sniper rifle and a .magnum bullet; cannot pick up revolvers.
You do not naturally lose san when alone.
Only spawns with a certain probability on specific maps.
Shop can purchase .magnum bullets, scopes, iron door keys.
Sniper rifle requires left‑click to load, load time 6 s; crouch + left‑click to attach scope, attach time 2 s.
When scope is not attached, right‑click fires directly; after firing you must wait 4 s before shooting again.
When scope is attached, crouch + right‑click toggles scope; without crouching right‑click fires directly.
Sniper rifle can only penetrate barriers and barrier panels.
When the sniper hits a player beyond 50 blocks it bypasses shields and kills them.
Special police die and drop a revolver.
info.screen.roleid.martial_arts_instruct:
Sheriff Camp
Start with nunchucks, cannot pick up revolvers.
Left‑click pushes the nearest player within 5 blocks to the left, right‑click pushes to the right.
Crouch + left‑click pulls the player to you, crouch + right‑click pushes the player away.
The same move cannot be used again within 7 s.
If this attack pushes the target onto a block, a second attack kills the target directly.
If within 7 s the same target receives 3 nunchuck hits, the target dies instantly.
After each nunchuck use you have 1 s cooldown; after each kill nunchucks have 7 s cooldown.
Using nunchucks three times in a row even without a kill puts them on 5 s cooldown.
Martial arts instructor drops a revolver upon death.
info.screen.roleid.glitch_robot:
Civilian Camp
You have no mood.
Has double stamina cap.
Immune to poison.
Every 30 s a malfunction occurs (stunned 3.5 s).
After death a faulty current remains (players in the current receive Slow 2).
Shop can purchase night‑vision goggles and glowstone dust.
When wearing night‑vision goggles, buying glowstone dust can charge the night‑vision device.
info.screen.roleid.nianshou:
Neutral Camp
Has an independent win condition.
You need to do tasks to maintain san, have 1.5× stamina, and appear good in killer vision.
Passive – Ghostly: When you are in darkness you receive a shield reagent (once per game), speed II, night vision; you can purchase turn‑off‑lights in shop.
Passive – Year Beast: While you exist, all players receive 4 firecrackers; if the year beast is surrounded (5‑block radius) by (2/3 of total players) firecrackers the year beast dies.
Active – Scatter Wealth: Every 2 tasks you receive a red packet; using skill on others to give a red packet makes them receive a prompt after 15 s and gain 100 gold.
Passive – Congratulations: When 5 minutes remain, all surviving players gain 100 gold and restore san.
Victory condition: If at game end all killers are dead and the year beast is alive (excluding gambler/recorder wins), the year beast wins.
info.screen.roleid.accountant:
Civilian Camp
You gain 25 gold every 60 s.
Crouch + skill key can toggle mode: income vs expense.
Press skill key directly to spend 175 gold to activate skill.
Income: Press skill key on a player to check if their stored gold exceeds 300 gold; if so you receive a message.
Expense: Mark a player; after 20 s you learn whether their gold increased or decreased.
Accountant shop can spend 100 gold to buy a passbook (only once).
Right‑click passbook consumes all your gold and stores it in the passbook.
Right‑click again to retrieve gold and consume the passbook.
Passbook drops when accountant dies.
info.screen.roleid.alchemist:
Civilian Camp
Crouch for 20 s to obtain a potion material.
Crouch + skill key switches the potion you are brewing.
Press skill key consuming 1 material + appropriate gold to brew a potion.
Each potion can be brewed twice; counts reset at game end.

Potions:
1. Adrenaline (100 gold): Increases stamina cap when used on target.
2. Antibiotic (100 gold): Removes poison from target.
3. Hedging‑Red (200 gold): Poisons target.
4. Dog‑skin Plaster (150 gold): Prevents san loss for target for 30 s.
info.screen.roleid.baka:
Civilian Camp
Bound with Town Problem‑Solver (Killer Camp).
You will not die from the forced exams of the Town Problem‑Solver.
You can close the Town Problem‑Solver’s exam page directly, but you still suffer san loss penalties.
You can spend 100 gold in the shop to buy a problem set.
Right‑click problem set to enter the solving interface; complete all problems within the time limit.
Success rewards 200 gold, otherwise san drops 30%%.
You can also give the problem set to other players.
Others who succeed get 100 gold; failure results in immediate death!
info.screen.roleid.maid_sakuya:
Civilian Camp
Easter‑egg role
You can purchase throwing knives. Throwing knives are fired with Q.
Can purchase Shiyu Night (costly), drinking it gives speed 10 for 20 s.
Spell Card: Illusion "The World"
Press skill key to activate; after use time stops for 5 s.
info.screen.roleid.pachuri:
Civilian Camp
Easter‑egg role
You can purchase a career book §d§lPachuri Knowledge Book§r.
It records a random player’s (including dead players) profession in the book.
info.screen.roleid.exampler:
Killer Camp
Bound with Cirno (Civilian Camp).

Active Skill 1 – Exam:
Spend 100 gold, select a player in inventory, force them to take an exam. You also must complete the test.
Player can spend 100 gold to cancel the death.

Active Skill 2 – All‑Out Competition:
All players start solving problems. Press skill key to activate, costs gold. Cooldown 240 s.
Both skills share a cooldown.
Each player who dies from forced exam gives you 1 energy.
Collect 3 energy to receive a berserk experience ticket; energy resets.
Berserk ticket can be exchanged in the shop to activate berserk mode.
info.screen.roleid.dio:
Killer Camp
Occupies 2 killer slots, bound with Jotaro (Sheriff Camp), will self‑ignite during day.
Shop can purchase throwing knives, power outage, lockpick.
Throwing knife: Q or right‑click charge to throw, ranged weapon.
Active Skill – Time Stop: Duration 10 s, cooldown 15 s, unlocks by feeding corpses.
During time stop, most players except you cannot move or use items.
Clocksmith and Maid Sakuya can move during time stop.

§lSpecial Passive§r
Final Carnival
Unlocks after feeding several corpses.
When hit, immune to death, gain 30 s temporary life.
If hit again during temporary life you die instantly.
Next corpse feed adds 30 s temporary life.
During temporary life speed +30%%.
info.screen.roleid.jojo:
Sheriff Camp
Bound with Dio (Killer Camp).
Special Skills
Platinum Star – World: Time stop 3 s, cooldown 240 s.
Passive: Gun cooldown doubled.
Platinum Star lets you open all doors.
Ripple Medal: Charge at least 2 s then punch, knock back nearby, dash forward.
Dash hitting target kills target with brain penalty. Cooldown 30 s.
Starts without a gun, time stop does not overwrite other time stops.
If Dio is within 3 blocks, Dio will be highlighted in your view.
Drops a revolver after death.
info.screen.roleid.locksmith:
Civilian Camp
Bound with Engineer (Civilian).
Can purchase screwdriver, crew key.
Screwdriver: Can repair doors broken by crowbars. Invisible while held.
Use screwdriver crouch + right‑click door to reset door (can be used when door is locked by lockpick) (and drops reinforced items).
info.screen.roleid.hoan_meirin:
Sheriff Camp
Easter‑egg role
Only spawns on jump‑enabled maps.
Press skill key to gain up to 10 s Float II. Cooldown 60 s.
Can press skill key again to stop.
Can punch with fists (left‑click).
Punching the same person 5 times kills them, brain penalty.
Starts with one layer of air shield; when broken it knocks back nearby players.

Passive – Fear of Solitude: If no one is within 5 blocks you will die after 60 s.
info.screen.roleid.ma_chen_xu:
Killer Camp
Only spawns on specific maps, occupies two killer slots.
When this killer is present all good camps gain a fear mechanic.

Fear Mechanic
Fear trigger: Good people near the bag‑ghost continuously lose SAN.
Evolution logic: When the bag‑ghost accumulates enough SAN loss from good people, it automatically unlocks the next ghost form.

Inner‑World Mechanic
All good people lose 5 SAN every 5 s, adding darkness.
[Soul‑Devour] effect: Bag‑ghost can left‑click kill players with SAN ≤ 10.
You can see players with SAN ≤ 10 in killer intuition, they appear cyan.
Each soul‑devour reduces inner‑world time by 10 s.

Shop can purchase rain (activates light rain, doubles SAN loss for all good people, grants one shield for 20 s).

Phase 1: Low‑level Ghost
Passive: Near good people within 10 blocks, every 10 s extra 5 SAN loss.
Unlock condition: Starts with it.
Evolution requirement: Accumulate extra 50 SAN loss.

Phase 2: Mid‑level Ghost
Self speed +10%%.
Randomly draw 1 ghost skill from pool.
Near good people within 10 blocks, every 10 s extra 5 SAN loss.

Phase 3: High‑level Ghost
Randomly draw 1 ghost skill.
Near good people within 15 blocks, every 10 s extra 7 SAN loss.
Unlock ultimate: Pray Rain.
Cost: 250 gold.
Effect: Force inner‑world for 30 s.

Phase 4: Ultimate Ghost
Randomly draw 1 ghost skill.
Self speed +10%%.
Gain 1 shield layer (blocks one lethal damage).

Ghost Skill Pool
Wind‑Sweep: Run 15 s to charge wind energy; use to increase speed by 50%% for 10 s, cooldown 20 s.
Puppet‑Play: Summon an identical puppet (appearance, held items); puppet walks autonomously, disappears on any damage. Puppet lasts 20 s then dies.
Mimic: One‑time per game, copy currently held item.
Silence: Freeze aimed target for 3 s, cooldown 30 s.
Blink: Dash toward mouse direction, gain 5 s slow.
Parasite: Aim at target, poison them; they die after 1 min.
info.screen.roleid.guest_ghost:
Civilian Camp
Only spawns on specific maps, bound with bag‑ghost.
Starts with 100 gold.

Pure Rain Symbol (active, CD 35 s)
Prevents bag‑ghost from triggering soul‑devour for 10 s.

Pray Clear
Unlock condition: Bag‑ghost has opened inner‑world.
Effect: Immediately close inner‑world and restore 20 SAN to all good people.
Shop can purchase peach‑wood nail.
Peach‑wood nail can put bag‑ghost’s ghost skill on cooldown for 30 s.
info.screen.roleid.sea_king:
Sheriff Camp
Only spawns on underwater maps.
Unlimited stamina.
Start with a trident of Loyalty 3, cannot pick up revolvers.
Has underwater breathing, and automatically gains Dolphin's Grace in water.
Skill: Press skill key to apply 5s Ghost Curse to players in water within a spherical radius of 20 blocks.
Shop can spend 150 gold to buy an extra trident; hitting a player with the trident kills them.
Sea King drops a revolver upon death.
info.screen.roleid.water_ghost:
Killer Camp
Only spawns on underwater maps.
Has underwater breathing.
Starts with a rush‑2 trident.
Only a trident‑sprint can kill a target.
Skill: Press skill key to gain 10 s Dolphin Blessing 1, cooldown 40 s.
Passive: After a riptide trident kill, the trident enters a 30s cooldown.
Shop can purchase lockpick, rain.
Passive: Water ghost dries out and dies after 35 s on land!
Water ghost drops trident as revolver upon death.
info.screen.roleid.diver:
Civilian Camp
Only spawns on underwater maps.
Unlimited stamina.
Has underwater breathing.
Shop can purchase:
Diving helmet: Provides underwater breathing and Dolphin Blessing 1; right‑click to throw to another player.
Diving boots: Comes with Deep‑Sea Explorer 3.
Diver can press skill key to remove equipment.
info.screen.modifier.lovers:
Modifier
You will be bound with another random player as lovers (lover assignment is independent of camp).
When one lover dies the other commits suicide.
If only you and your lover survive to the end, lovers win.
To achieve victory you may kill your teammates (specifically killers).
info.screen.modifier.refugee:
Modifier
Players with the refugee modifier will respawn as outlaws 120 s after death and enter outlaw mode.
After becoming an outlaw you must kill all players to win.
If you are killed after becoming an outlaw, game time/item slots/gold/sanity revert to the moment of outlaw respawn.
All players killed during outlaw period will respawn.
During outlaw period, some roles, modifiers’ active/passive abilities become unavailable.
Examples:
- Post‑mortem killer’s corpse and death immunity, Necromancer resurrection, Avenger revenge, Admirer observation, Vulture corpse eating
- Puppeteer corpse eating, Beginner transformation, Amnesiac corpse selection, Split Personality active switch (passive still works)
- Red‑Dust Guest phase switch (except win), Sneaker advanced stage 2, Thief skill, Patrol guard passive
info.screen.modifier.feather:
Modifier
You have no collision volume.
info.screen.modifier.taskmaster:
Modifier
Killer/Neutral/Civilian players gain extra gold after completing tasks.
info.screen.modifier.allergist:
Modifier
When players eat/drink there is a chance to trigger one of the following effects.
33%% nothing happens, 33%% immunity to one poison, 33%% speed 2, 1%% death
info.screen.modifier.cursed:
Modifier
Gain a random potion effect every 30 s.
When you die this modifier is transferred to your killer.
info.screen.modifier.secretive:
Modifier
Others cannot obtain information from your corpse.
info.screen.modifier.knight:
Modifier
When you are in darkness there is a chance to teleport to another player's position.
info.screen.modifier.split_personality:
Modifier
Two people share one body, switching personality every 60 s or manually.
Default skin is the first personality.
The other personality can hear the current one's voice and see the screen.
When the body dies both personalities must choose “Deceive” or “Sacrifice”.
If both choose “Deceive” the body dies immediately.
If one chooses “Deceive” and the other “Sacrifice”, the “Deceive” side revives, the “Sacrifice” side dies.
If both choose “Sacrifice”, both revive but only live for 60 s and keep their inventories.
§c§lIf you die during refugee time you will not revive.§r
info.screen.modifier.expedition:
Modifier
When only 2 good people remain your role switches to Red Navy.
Only effective in civilian camp.
info.screen.modifier.introverted:
Modifier
You dislike being in crowds.
If more than 2 players are within 5 blocks, your san drops faster.
If you are alone or only one player is nearby, your san does not drop.
info.screen.modifier.taxed:
Modifier
Your gold gain from kills and passive income is reduced by 25%%.
